coin_profile.name: Cardano
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Cardano is a proof-of-stake Layer 1 blockchain built on peer-reviewed academic research. It uses the Ouroboros consensus mechanism and is developed by IOHK. Cardano focuses on scalability, sustainability, and interoperability, with significant adoption in developing-world identity and financial inclusion projects.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? coin_profile.description: Avalanche is a high-speed, low-cost Layer 1 platform known for its subnet architecture, which allows anyone to launch custom blockchains. moat.rationale: AVAX has moderate competitive defensibility with a proven L1 design, but faces intense competition from SOL, ARB, and OP with no clear technical superiority—it has been forked and lacks ETH's settlement dominance. Network effects exist through the Avalanche ecosystem, but developer and user lock-in remains weak as switching costs are low and multi-chain deployment is standard practice. Token utility is strong on holder value accrual (10/10) via staking/validation, yet capital efficiency (5/10) and dilution control (4/10) are concerning, indicating moderate real economic value capture that doesn't translate to deep moat protection against competing L1s.
